concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: LIFO
concepts.short_definition: The principle where the last element added to a structure is the first one to be removed.

concepts.name: Push operation
concepts.short_definition: The operation of adding an element to the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Pop operation
concepts.short_definition: The operation of removing and returning the element from the top of the stack.

concepts.name: Peek operation
concepts.short_definition: The operation of viewing the element at the top of the stack without removing it.

concepts.name: Empty stack
concepts.short_definition: A stack that contains no elements.

concepts.name: Full stack
concepts.short_definition: A stack that has reached its maximum capacity (relevant for fixed-size implementations).

concepts.name: Stack implementation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for realizing a stack data structure, typically using arrays or linked lists.

concepts.name: Abstract Data Type
concepts.short_definition: A logical description of a data structure, defining its operations and behavior without specifying implementation details.

concepts.name: Applications of Stack
concepts.short_definition: Real-world scenarios and problems where stacks are effectively utilized.
